title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: An international student exchange programme has asked you to write a report on the experience of studying abroad. Your report should describe the main benefits of the experience, identify any challenges students typically face, and make recommendations for how the programme could better prepare participants.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on the Student Exchange Programme Experience

Introduction
This report is based on interviews conducted with twelve students who participated in the international exchange programme during the previous academic year. It outlines the principal benefits and challenges of the experience and proposes improvements to the pre-departure preparation process.

Benefits of Studying Abroad
Participants consistently reported that the exchange programme had a transformative impact on their personal development. Immersion in a different academic culture broadened their intellectual perspectives and encouraged greater independence. Language skills improved dramatically when students were required to use a foreign language for all daily interactions. Several participants also noted that the international connections they formed proved professionally valuable upon graduation.

Challenges
The most common difficulties were:
- Initial language barriers that made academic integration slower than expected.
- Feelings of homesickness and cultural isolation during the first month, before social networks were established.
- Inconsistencies in academic credit transfer, which caused administrative complications on return.

Recommendations
To improve participants' readiness, the programme should consider:
- Introducing a mandatory language preparedness course in the six months before departure.
- Pairing incoming students with a local peer mentor for the first four weeks.
- Establishing a clearer and more transparent credit equivalency framework in collaboration with partner institutions.

Conclusion
The exchange programme offers exceptional developmental opportunities. With stronger pre-departure support, its benefits would be accessible and enjoyable from the very first day of arrival.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You have recently moved to a new city and are looking for a local sports club or social group to join. You have found a website for a hiking and outdoor activities club. Write an email to the club secretary enquiring about membership, asking about the activities they organise, and explaining why you think the club would be a good fit for you.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Club Secretary,

I am writing to enquire about membership of the Westbrook Hiking and Outdoor Activities Club, having come across your website while searching for social activities in the area. I recently relocated to Westbrook for work and am eager to explore the local countryside while meeting new people.

I have been a keen hiker for approximately five years, having regularly walked trails in the Scottish Highlands and the Lake District. I would describe my fitness level as good and my walking experience as intermediate—comfortable with full-day routes of up to twenty kilometres over varied terrain, though not yet experienced in overnight expeditions.

I would be grateful if you could provide some information about the types of activities the club organises throughout the year. Specifically, I am interested in knowing how frequently walks are arranged, whether they are graded by difficulty, and if there are any organised social events alongside the physical activities. I am also curious about whether the club engages in any environmental or conservation volunteering, as this is something I would be very willing to participate in.

I believe the club would be an excellent match for my interests and a wonderful way to settle into life in Westbrook. I would be happy to attend a trial walk before committing to full membership, if that is something you offer.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to your reply.

Kind regards,
Simon Adeola

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen the following notice on an online travel forum: 'We want to hear about hotels or accommodation that truly impressed you—positively or negatively. Describe your stay, the service, and whether you would recommend it.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
An Unexpected Treasure: The Moorland Inn

Booked on impulse during a last-minute weekend trip to the Derbyshire Peaks, the Moorland Inn was a discovery that immediately erased any concern about the spontaneity of our plans. What greeted us was an experience that felt less like a hotel stay and more like being welcomed into a very comfortable home.

The inn is a converted eighteenth-century farmhouse that has been restored with a rare combination of historical sensitivity and modern comfort. The twelve guest rooms are individually furnished with antique pieces, yet each contains the practical amenities—excellent wifi, powerful showers, and genuinely good mattresses—that travellers actually require. The owners, who manage the property themselves, strike the ideal balance between attentive hospitality and respecting guests' privacy.

Breakfast deserves special mention. Served in a low-ceilinged dining room with original stone flagging, it consists entirely of locally sourced produce. The eggs are from hens kept in the grounds; the bread is baked on site each morning. It was the finest hotel breakfast I have encountered in years, and alone worth the modest premium over chain accommodation.

The only limitation I can honestly note is that the inn is not suitable for guests who require extensive amenities such as a pool or spa. It offers a peaceful rural retreat rather than a resort experience.

For travellers seeking genuine character, warm hospitality, and a deeply restorative escape from urban life, the Moorland Inn is, quite simply, exceptional.
